candidateFindings.pageTitle: Visiting Student Rotation | Howard University College of Medicine
candidateFindings.shortQuote: All applications are processed based on the month applied and are approved on a first-come, first serve basis

negativeEvidence.strongNegativeEvidence: VSLO platform itself restricts applicants to AAMC-member LCME-accredited and AOA/COCA-accredited osteopathic schools, so the VSLO requirement excludes IMG / international (non-US) medical students from this lane. No international-student-specific lane found on medicine.howard.edu.
negativeEvidence.weakNegativeEvidence: huhealthcare.com (hospital clinical site) does not surface a visiting-students lane directly; the lane lives on the medical-school subdomain. Schedules Booklet PDF holds the verbatim audience paragraph but PDF text extraction failed this turn.

nextAction: Eligible for future public-safe activation with caveats: (a) DEPARTMENT_LEVEL_SOURCE — Howard COM Dermatology visiting-students rotation; hub-level visiting-students page not located on medicine.howard.edu in this sprint; (b) VSLO required (verbatim); (c) audience inferred from VSLO platform policy (LCME/AOA); (d) HUH placement implicit but not verbatim-named; (e) cost and visa NOT_STATED. Manual retry queued in `p101_manual_retry_log.csv` to capture: (i) verbatim audience paragraph from Schedules Booklet PDF, (ii) verbatim HUH placement-site naming, (iii) cost and visa language if any.
plainEnglishSummary: Howard University College of Medicine accepts visiting medical students for clinical rotations including Dermatology; applications go through VSLO. Because VSLO is AAMC's platform for AAMC-member US LCME and AOA-accredited schools, this lane is effectively for US MD/DO students only. Howard University Hospital is Howard COM's teaching hospital so it is the implicit placement site, but the verbatim naming and the verbatim audience paragraph live in the Schedules Booklet PDF that did not extract cleanly this turn. Queued for manual retry. Cost and visa not stated on the public source.
driftCheck: This advanced national USCE discovery because DC gained a third classified institution (closing the DC thin-coverage gap from 1 to 4 institutions in P101-0 alone); negative evidence about Howard's PDF-only audience documentation is captured rather than fabricated, preserving the core quality law.
